claim: For all integers n > 1, n is prime if and only if (n-1)! + 1 is divisible by n.
If n is prime and n > 5, then n divides (n-2)! - 1.
If n is prime and n > 3, then (n+1)! + 1 is divisible by n.
If n is prime and n > 5, then (n-2)! + (n-1)! ≡ 0 mod n.
If n is prime and n > 3, then (n+1)! - (n-1)! ≡ 2 mod n.
If n is prime and n > 2, then n divides (n-1)! + 1 and also divides 2^{n-1} - 1.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (not is_prime(n)) or (((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and ((pow(2, n-1, n) - 1) % n == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 2, then gcd((n-1)! + 1, 2^{n-1} - 1) = n.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (not is_prime(n)) or (gcd(factorial(n-1) + 1, pow(2, n-1) - 1) == n)

claim: If n is an odd prime, then 2^{n-1} ≡ 1 mod n and (n-1)! ≡ -1 mod n imply n divides 2^{n-1} + (n-1)! + 2.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (n % 2 == 0) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((pow(2, n-1, n) == 1) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and ((pow(2, n-1) + factorial(n-1) + 2) % n == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 2, then n divides (n-1)! + 2^{n-1} + 2.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (not is_prime(n)) or (((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and ((pow(2, n-1) + 2) % n == 0) and ((factorial(n-1) + pow(2, n-1) + 2) % n == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 7, then (n-3)! + 1 is divisible by n.
If n is prime and n > 7, then (n-3)! + (n-1)! + 2 is divisible by n.
predicate: (n <= 7) or (not is_prime(n)) or (((factorial(n-3) + 1) % n == 0) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and ((factorial(n-3) + factorial(n-1) + 2) % n == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 5, then n divides sigma(n-1) - tau(n-1).
If n is prime and n > 5, then sigma(n-1) ≡ tau(n-1) mod n.
If n is prime and n > 2, then phi(n-1) is even.
If n is prime and n > 3, then phi(n-1) is divisible by 4.
If n is prime and n > 5, then phi(n-1) is divisible by 8 if n ≡ 1 mod 8.
If n is prime and n > 2, then tau(n-1) is odd if and only if n-1 is a square.
If n is prime and n > 3, then tau(n-1) is even unless n-1 is a square.
If n is prime and n > 5, then gcd(phi(n-1), tau(n-1)) > 1.
If n is prime and n > 5, then phi(n-1) and tau(n-1) share a common prime factor.
predicate: (n <= 5) or (not is_prime(n)) or (any(p for p in range(2, min(phi(n-1), tau(n-1))+1) if phi(n-1)%p==0 and tau(n-1)%p==0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 3, then n divides sigma(n-1) + phi(n-1).
If n is prime and n > 3, then sigma(n-1) + phi(n-1) ≡ 0 mod n.
If n is prime and n > 2, then n divides sigma(n) - n - 1.
If n is prime and n > 2, then sigma(n) ≡ n+1 mod n^2.
If n is prime and n > 2, then sigma(n) - n - 1 = 0 and (n-1)! + 1 ≡ 0 mod n.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((sigma(n) - n - 1 == 0) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 7, then vp(n-1,2) >= vp(n-1,3).
If n is prime and n > 7, then the exponent of 2 in n-1 is at least as large as that of 3, and n-1 has at least 4 divisors.
predicate: (n <= 7) or (not is_prime(n)) or (vp(n-1,2) >= vp(n-1,3) and tau(n-1) >= 4)

claim: If n is prime and n > 5, then n divides sigma(n-1) - phi(n-1) - tau(n-1).
predicate: (n <= 5) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((sigma(n-1) - phi(n-1) - tau(n-1)) % n == 0)

claim: If n is prime and n > 5, then 2*sigma(n-1) ≡ phi(n-1) + tau(n-1) mod n.
predicate: (n <= 5) or (not is_prime(n)) or ((2*sigma(n-1) - phi(n-1) - tau(n-1)) % n == 0)

claim: If n is prime and n > 3, then n divides (n-1)! + n - 1.
If n is prime and n > 3, then (n-1)! + n - 1 ≡ 0 mod n and (n-1)! + 1 ≡ 0 mod n imply n divides n-2.
predicate: (n <= 3) or (not is_prime(n)) or (((factorial(n-1) + n - 1) % n == 0) and ((factorial(n-1) + 1) % n == 0) and ((n-2) % n == 0))

claim: If n is prime and n > 2, then phi(n) = n-1 and tau(n) = 2.
predicate: (n <= 2) or (not is_prime(n)) or (phi(n) == n-1 and tau(n) == 2)

claim: If n is prime and n > 2, then phi(n) + tau(n) = n + 1.
If n is prime and n > 2, then phi(n) * tau(n) = 2*(n-1).
If n is prime and n > 2, then 2*(n-1) + (n+1) = 3n - 1.
If n is prime and n > 5, then n divides the sum of divisors of (n-2).
If n is prime and n > 5, then sigma(n-1) + sigma(n-2) ≡ 0 mod n.
If n is prime and n > 3, then n divides the product (n-1)*(n-2).
If n is prime and n > 3, then (n-1)*(n-2) + (n-1)! + n - 1 ≡ 0 mod n.
predicate: (n <= 3) or (not is_prime(n)) or (((n-1)*(n-2) + factorial(n-1) + n - 1) % n == 0)

claim: If n is prime and n > 7, then n divides phi(n-1) + sigma(n-1).
If n is prime and n > 7, then phi(n-1) + sigma(n-1) ≡ 2*tau(n-1) mod n.
